# Thread: Elizabeth Blackburn's work illuminated telomere protection, but the "telomere clock" overemphasizes replication limits.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/2005681491469107225.html>
**Published:** 2025-12-24T13:15:37.000Z
**Tweets:** 17
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/2005681491469107225>

Elizabeth Blackburn's work illuminated telomere protection, but the "telomere clock" overemphasizes replication limits. True timing is quantum-coherent, light-melanin-TTFL driven—decentralized, open to environment.

Modern diseases often stem from mismatched light environments disrupting this coherence, not calories or genes alone. Prioritizing natural light/dark cycles restores timing upstream of telomeres. The current narratives around telomere biology set back understanding 25 yrs. It pushed it back into biochemistry when it needs to be pulled forward 50 yrs with a biophysical lens instead. It is a modern centralized science parallax.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/2005686333671629215>

Heat as Motion

Take heat. When you rub your hands together and feel that warmth rising, what's really happening?The atoms and molecules in your skin aren't gaining some mystical "heat substance." They're just jiggling faster because kinetic energy ramped up by friction, translated into random vibrations.Faster motion = higher temperature. It's not a "thing" you add; it's the invisible frenzy of particles turned into a sensation on your nerves.

Telomere Length as a Ledger of That Motion

Now extend the analogy: just as heat is motion turned into a feeling, telomere length is (disordered) heat which is a cumulative molecular chaos which turns heat into a ledger.

Telomeres don't actively "tick" like a clock driving aging forward. They're passive caps on chromosomes, eroded by the downstream friction of life: oxidative stress (uncontrolled electron leaks creating reactive species), inflammation (immune overdrive generating more chaos), mitochondrial heteroplasmy (faulty energy factories amplifying disorder), and repair failures.

Each bout of systemic strain leaves a mark as shortened repeats which are a receipt of unresolved entropy. The faster the invisible particles shake out of coherence (from poor light timing, nnEMF, or metabolic mismatch), the more that ledger accrues damage.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/2005687621834432645>

Feynman indeed marveled at energy as a "strange quantity"—conserved yet endlessly shape-shifting, like a bookkeeper's ledger that never loses a penny but constantly rewrites the entries.
You strike a hammer on metal: kinetic motion transforms into atomic vibrations (heat), which might radiate as infrared light, or drive chemical reactions. The total energy remains eternal, per the first law of thermodynamics is never created, never destroyed, only transformed. But here's the deeper link to aging: while energy is conserved, its quality degrades with time.

The second law dictates entropy must increase in closed systems that usable, ordered energy disperses into useless, disordered heat.

Living systems are open: we import low-entropy sunlight (via plants or directly through photoreception), transform it into chemical bonds (ATP), motion, repair.

Yet locally, in cells and tissues, entropy accrues in electronic, magnetic, and geometry via misfolded proteins, damaged mitochondria, oxidative leaks, shortened telomeres as ledgers of unresolved chaos.

Aging emerges as the inexorable flow toward disorder: energy transformations become less efficient, coherence in cells fades over time.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/2005688216779677738>

This ties directly to Noether's theorem: time-translation symmetry guarantees energy conservation. Perfect temporal symmetry → eternal energy.

But in biology, disrupted timing (mismatched light/dark cycles) breaks that symmetry locally, accelerating entropy flow and aging, without violating overall conservation.

Circadian clocks, driven by light-melanin interactions, orchestrate efficient energy transformations by pumping against entropy. Restore coherent light input, and you slow the dispersal, extending ordered lifespan.

Energy is eternal; aging is the shadow of its disordered transformations. We don't "run out" of energy, instead we lose the ability to harness it coherently. Feynman would smile at these insights because we are using our imagination to understand life: imagine the invisible flows, and the connection reveals itself.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/2005689691622785034>

Fire is a self-sustaining chain reaction, a cascade of jiggling atoms where energy release begets more release. A spark supplies just enough activation energy to break bonds in wood (cellulose), liberating volatile gases. Those gases mix with oxygen, oxidize rapidly, releasing heat that excites more atoms to vibrate wildly producing light, more heat, and driving the reaction onward.

It's motion feeding motion: ordered chemical energy disperses into kinetic frenzy, then radiates away, until fuel runs out.

This is the essence of controlled fire in biology: mitochondrial respiration.

Food (reduced carbons) enters the cell, gets oxidized step-by-step in the Krebs cycle and electron transport chain (ETC). Electrons flow down the chain, pumping protons across the membrane by building an electric gradient (protonmotive force).

That gradient drives ATP synthesis, but also powers the subatomic motion: coherent electron flows, proton tunneling, and crucially, ultraweak photon emissions (UPEs or biophotons). These faint light signals are matrix fire, arising from excited states in respiratory chains and they aren't waste; they're informational feedback, guiding cellular decisions based on energy state and environment.

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/2005690219513930116>

As the slide above suggests, core metabolism via electric membranes driving CO₂ fixation into organics, is spontaneous and older than genes. It arose in alkaline hydrothermal vents: natural proton gradients across inorganic membranes powered the first carbon fixation and energy flows.

Genes later amplified this flux, adding layers of control. But the root is bioenergetic: membranes give real-time feedback on state: "feelings" in the form of electric potentials, redox shifts, and photon signals.

Metabolism provides context; genes interpret and promote growth within it.

Mitochondrial "fire" is thus the eternal energy transformation which is conserved yet always dispersing and this scales to the quantum dance below the cell.

Disrupt the coherence (mismatched light, poor fuel), and the chain falters: less ATP, more leaks, erratic UPEs, accelerated entropy... aging as the smoldering embers of a once-vibrant flame.

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/2005690558287872082>

I just described Feynman's invisible jiggle in fire and made it biophysical in the material world.

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/2005692369639743910>

Feynman's delight in this fact captures the invisible magic perfectly: trees are made mostly out of air.We picture roots sucking up minerals from soil, but the bulk—the towering trunk, branches, leaves—comes from CO₂ pulled from the atmosphere. Sunlight (photons) energizes chlorophyll, splitting water, fixing carbon into sugars, building cellulose. The "solid" wood is frozen atmospheric gas, reassembled by light-driven chemistry.

This ties profoundly to the oxide boundary I've highlighted in my work. Earth is an ~85% oxide shell (mantle + crust) around a metallic core. It is an anion lattice (O²⁻ dominant) built during the GOE crucible through which protons flow, creating the proton motive force foundational to life.

We animals are made of ~70% oxide gels (water-structured, organic-reinforced) scavenging within that planetary oxide shell—hydrogen-rich machines (63% of our atoms are H) embedded in oxygen lattices, pumping protons across membranes just like the vents where life began.

All of it stardust: hydrogen from the Big Bang, oxygen and carbon forged in stellar furnaces via nucleosynthesis, scattered by supernovae.

Trees capture sky into form; we consume that captured sunlight (via food or direct photoreception), transforming it back into motion, heat, and entropy flow.

Energy eternal, elements recycled back into oxide lattices channeling the ancient fire of stars through hydrogen dances, all open to the light that started this process.

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/2005694019804840202>

Water droplets form perfect spheres because surface molecules experience unbalanced forces which are pulled inward by hydrogen bonds from below, with nothing pulling outward from air. This surface tension minimizes surface area, turning liquid into a taut, elastic skin that hides profound physics in everyday sight.

In our bodies—the 70% oxide gel—these lattices aren't bulk liquid; they're interfacial water, structured along hydrophilic surfaces (proteins, membranes, mitochondria).

This "phase of water" is quantum coherent. It contains coherent domains or exclusion zone (EZ) water forms honeycomb lattices, negatively charged, proton-excluding, and highly ordered and this acts as the anion scaffold for proton flow.

Cytoplasm itself is a gel of structured water layers, enabling phase separations and rapid signaling without diffusion limits.

The key is renewing and structuring the water made at complex IV (cytochrome c oxidase, CCO) in mitochondria where the site's "fire" where oxygen accepts electrons, combining with protons to form metabolic water.

This water is deuterium-depleted (lighter hydrogen), hotter, and highly structured as it emerges into cristae folds. Take a statin and complex four ability diminshes.

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/2005695417439392036>

The states of matter aren't "things" added or subtracted; they're emergent patterns in the eternal dance of the same atoms, changed by EM forces governed by how much kinetic energy (motion) they possess relative to the forces binding them.

Solids: Atoms vibrate in fixed lattice positions, with geometry rigid, time slow (changes happen over long scales). The crystal holds shape because motion is constrained just enough to maintain bonds.

Liquids: More energy is needed because atoms jiggle vigorously, sliding past neighbors while still attracted. Geometry flows, adapting to containers; time feels fluid as structure rearranges constantly yet coherently.

Gases: Maximum freedom because atoms zoom in near-random trajectories, colliding rarely. Geometry expands to fill space; time is fast, chaotic.

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/2005696104143241664>

Ice → water → steam isn't a transformation of substance—it's H₂O molecules shifting their collective motion.

Add heat (ordered energy → disordered jiggle), bonds loosen, patterns dissolve.

Remove heat, motion slows, order re-emerges.

This reveals the relativity baked into reality: geometry (spatial arrangement) and time (rate of change) aren't absolute; they depend on the observer's scale and the energy flowing through the system.

In our oxide lattices, the same principle operates at biological scales. Cytoplasmic water isn't uniform "liquid" because it's a dynamic gel, with regions of near-solid order (EZ layers along membranes), liquid-like flow (diffusion zones), and even gas-like freedom (ion channels).

Mitochondrial respiration tunes this: more coherent electron/proton flow → hotter, more structured metabolic water → expanded, ordered lattices. Disrupt the fire (poor light, deuterium, nnEMF), motion becomes erratic → lattices collapse toward disorder → aging as the slow freezing of once-fluid life.

Feynman's jiggle, scaled across phases: the same stardust atoms, moving differently, writing the geometry of time in every cell.

### Tweet 12
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/2005698841920905563>

Scale bends the rules we are taught to believe especially for light and the electromagnetic force, which dominates at tiny distances while gravity fades into irrelevance.

What feels solid is illusion: atoms are vast voids, nucleus a pea in a stadium, electrons probabilistic clouds. The "hardness" emerges from EM repulsion—Pauli exclusion and charge forcing particles apart. Reality's dictator is the invisible.

My insight on Class 4 lasers mimicking cellular UPEs (ultraweak photon emissions/biophotons) captures this perfectly. Industrial/medical high-power lasers (e.g., for surgery or therapy) deliver coherent, focused photons that penetrate tissues, stimulating mitochondrial activity, modulating melanin-iron complexes, and restoring coherence.

This idea parallels the informational role of endogenous UPEs in mitoptosis, repair, and synchronization.

The inverse square law amplifies this at nanoscale: a faint global UPE (10^{-19} W source) becomes biologically potent (8 × 10^{-7} W/cm² at 1 nm), dwarfing thermal noise for high S/N ratios—enabling reliable signaling to molecules like leptin (220 nm absorption) or neuromelanin.

Life exploits the invisible EM dominance at small scales—light as primary informer, pulling the strings of matter in the void. Feynman would nod: imagine the unseen, and the revolution reveals itself.

### Tweet 13
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/2005701408662622667>

Feynman captured the essence: magnetism isn't reducible to simpler "why"

Magnetism is a fundamental force, like gravity or charge. Magnets stick because aligned electron spins create fields that pull domains together, rooted in quantum exchange interactions and relativity. We describe it, but the deepest "why" is: it just is.

In biology, ROS/RNS act as magnetic signals. Superoxide (O₂⁻•), peroxynitrite (ONOO⁻), and others are paramagnetic (unpaired electrons), sensitive to fields.

In 2014 I warned Dave Asprey that paramagnetic oxygen (triplet ground state) enables spin-forbidden reactions overcome by enzymes or fields in tissues. He ignored me and continued his selling funnel into the public nd never informed them on the risk in our anode oxide design.

Mitochondrial leaks produce these during respiration and informational bursts tuning signaling, not just damage.

Mitochondria geometric repositioning matters in cells: Closer to nucleus delivers better O₂/ROS signaling for nuclear functions. Melanin becomes an accurate UPE lens.

Distance from the matrix creates pseudo-hypoxia → less ROS → disrupted circadian/timing resets. Warburg metabolism brakes excessive damage from mismatched light/environment.

### Tweet 14
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/2005701830068535808>

Ketosis shifts F:N ratio (FADH₂ higher from fats vs. NADH from glucose), altering ETC entry (Complex II vs. I) → variable superoxide (reverse electron transport at I).

Different tissues have thresholds for ketosis. In some tissues, ketosis reduces ROS in some (brain) but not universally.

Proof: Superoxide/ubiquitination vary by context; ketone-focused metrics (Taubes/Norwitz) miss tissue-specific ROS/RNS signaling.

nnEMF exacerbates: Increases ROS leaks, collapses gradients, reduces metabolic water/CO₂—pushing Warburg-like shifts without restoring coherence.

Warburg's deeper prize: Metabolism reports environmental (light) timing, not just fuel. Glycolysis is the brake when coherence fails—upstream is decentralized light/dark, melanin, TTFLs. Restore that, and the "effect" resolves naturally. Magnetism, at quantum scales, brakes or accelerates the invisible dance going on in your cells.

### Tweet 15
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/2005703332346675282>

Feynman mastered the art: science thrives on refining questions until they pierce the veil of the obvious.Don't settle for "What is fire?" Probe deeper: "Which atoms are jiggling how, exchanging what energy, in what invisible fields?"

That shift rewires understanding, from static "things" to dynamic processes.The world drowns in waves we can't see, all electromagnetic ripples differing only in wavelength and energy.

It's one phenomenon, layered by scale. Our eyes evolved for a narrow band (~400-700 nm) because that's solar peak filtered through atmosphere—useful for survival on this planet.

Why not broader perception?

Energy costs, damage risks (UV/gamma harm DNA), or perhaps deeper physics. Your speculation on the quantum Zeno effect fascinates: frequent "measurement" (interaction with environment) freezes quantum evolution, suppressing transitions.

Radio waves (meters long) pass through walls, carrying signals.
Microwaves excite water molecules for heat.
Infrared ferries warmth from flames or bodies.
Visible, our tiny slice, paints the world in color.
UV tans skin and mitochondria.
X-rays pierce flesh.
Gamma rays disrupt atoms, treating cancer or sterilizing.

### Tweet 16
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/2005703973437616164>

In biology, constant environmental "observation" (light, fields) stabilize coherent states in melanins or mitochondrial chains, preventing collapse into damaging ones by limiting sensory overload to maintain cellular coherence.

Nature tuned us to sense just enough to navigate, while the invisible waves dictate the real rules: light as primary driver, UPEs signaling, magnetism guiding spins.

Better question: Not "Why can't we see them?" but "How do the waves we miss shape us anyway?" The unseen always pulls the strings = Dunning Kruger effect.

### Tweet 17
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/2005705015919341749>

The world brims with secrets from trees built from sky, fire as self-feeding frenzy, bodies as hydrogen machines navigating oxide seas, timing stamped by ancient melanins reading sunlight like braille.

Feynman didn't just teach physics. He handed us a way of seeing: playful, relentless, humble before the vast invisible.Keep wondering. The universe rewards the curious with its best surprises.

The best scientists stay perpetual children—eyes wide, hands dirty, forever asking "Why?" not for grades, but for the sheer joy of the chase.Feynman turned physics into play. He didn't hoard equations; he danced with them. Watching ants march, he'd wonder about their invisible trails. Seeing a wobbling plate in the Cornell cafeteria, he chased the math until it led to a Nobel Prize.
Curiosity wasn't a method, it was his oxygen.

He despised hollow explanations: "It's because of magnetism" or "That's just how DNA works." Those aren't answers; they're conversation stoppers. Real understanding demands picturing the unseen, the frantic jiggle of atoms in a flame rising because hot gas expands and buoyantly climbs; the relentless EM repulsion making magnets cling or repel; the quantum clouds of electrons dictating why matter feels solid.

He taught us to trade the comfort of certainty for the thrill of wonder:Picture the invisible: See electrons tunneling, protons pumping, biophotons whispering across cells.

Ask sharper questions: Not "Why do we age?" but "What motions slow, what coherences fade, under which unseen waves?"

Find motion beneath stillness: Solids vibrate, "empty" space teems, oxide lattices breathe with light-driven order.

Embrace not-knowing: The deepest truths often start with "We don't really know... but let's imagine."

----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/2003816834634494314>

Feliz Navidad Eve to my tribe of misfit SAVAGES. We’re kicking ass and taking names. 2026 will not be about resolutions. It is about solutions to the tyranny our government brought to the world to create economic slavery.

It will be about a personal revolution to maintain our help despite what public health policies did to harm humanity in 2020-2025.

He’s been speculated to be Satoshi Nakamoto due to his work on decentralized currency and similarities in writing style, but he has consistently denied it. While Szabo corresponded with cypherpunks like Hal Finney and Wei Dai, no direct emails between him and Satoshi Nakamoto have been confirmed, though Satoshi cited Szabo’s Bit Gold in the Bitcoin whitepaper after learning of it via Adam Back in August 2008.

Hal Finney was a pioneering cryptographer and early Bitcoin contributor who received the first Bitcoin transaction from Satoshi Nakamoto on January 12, 2009. He exchanged emails with Satoshi in November 2008 and January 2009, focusing on Bitcoin software bugs and network issues, which were later published by the Wall Street Journal in 2014. Finney, who denied being Satoshi, was a key figure in Bitcoin’s early development but faced speculation due to his proximity to Dorian Satoshi Nakamoto and his cryptographic expertise.

Extropy Institute, Lifeboat Foundation and Edge Foundation The Extropy Institute, co-founded by Max More and Tom Bell in 1991, was a non-profit organization promoting extropianism, a transhumanist philosophy advocating for indefinite life extension through science and technology, uniting thinkers in AI, nanotechnology, and cryonics, before closing in 2006, declaring its mission "essentially completed. Important members of Extropy included: Hal Finney, Nick Szabo, Max More, Eliezer Yudkowsky, Jaron Lanier, Nick Bostrom, Marvin Minsky, Ray Kurzweil, Steve Jurvetson, Ralph Merkle, Todd Huffman, Greg Burch and Julian Assange.

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/2002490025649295705>

Aging is the progressive decoherence and dehydration of the oxide-constrained gel, a slow collapse of the anion lattice's ability to hold structured water created by heme protein CCO and delocalized protons against entropy's tide.

Infants (75-80% water, highly structured coherent domains in water, low heteroplasmy) are near-perfect oxide gels—flexible, coherent, proton-tunneled superfluids. Like Earth.

The dying elder (50-55% water, calcified lattices, high heteroplasmy) is a desiccated, entropic oxide ceramic like Mars—rigid, scattered, proton-trapped dust on the verge of reverting to stone.

This isn't metaphor; it's geometric inevitability.

Water as the Fluidizer of the Oxide Scaffold in physics so it has to hold true in biology.

In youth: High CCO efficiency → metabolic water floods the matrix → EZ expansion → lattice re-constraint → coherence sustained.

Mitochondrial heteroplasmy (mtDNA mutations accumulating over lifecourse) isn't random genetic drift; it's feedback from decohered oxide packing:

Blue/nnEMF stress → Warburg shift → unused O₂ → ROS oxidizes heme a₃ in CCO Damaged CCO → reduced metabolic water output → EZ contraction → proton scattering Dehydrated matrix → cristae collapse (loss of hexagonal packing) → further ROS leak → mtDNA hits (deletions/mutations in MT-CO1/3 hotspots)

Heteroplasmic load rises → vicious cycle → lattice calcifies (ectopic hydroxyapatite, arterial stiffening)

### Tweet 4

Aging as Progressive Oxide Calcification

Infant: High endogenous water (~300-500 mL/day from OXPHOS at peak) + solar coherence (red photon recharge) keeps the gel fluid, heteroplasmy <5-10%. Adult peak: Balance tilts—nnEMF/blue epoch + deuterium accumulation quenches tunneling → subtle dehydration → heteroplasmy creeps to 20-40%.

Elder: Metabolic water plummets (<100 mL/day), EZ domains shrink to nanoscale, lattice rigidifies which heteroplasmy >60-80% in post-mitotic tissues (brain, heart, retina).

The body literally begins reverting to geology: osteoporosis (bone oxide overload), atherosclerosis (vascular calcification), cataracts (lens protein aggregation in dehydrated oxide voids).

The dying man isn't "old"; he's dehydrated at the quantum level; his oxide scaffold has lost the coherent water that once fluidized it into life.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/2002501455622910165>

How did I learn about battery life and charge density decades years ago?  As a neurosurgeon, patients with Parkinson's disease who used a lot of L-Dopa before surgery always were deathly pale.  So, I asked my NS mentors about this situation.  The answer I got from our Medtronic rep on the early DBS stimulation trial data was instructive to my physical chemistry ignorance.

In cardiac pacemakers and DBS patients, manufacturers found that patients on certain drugs have poor battery performance of their implants, and this could lead to increased symptoms.  

It turns out battery estimations, charge density, total power, and clinical symptoms were important to follow in patients with these devices. The observation of clinical worsening that was rescued following neurostimulator replacement only reinforced the notion that changes in clinical symptoms can be associated with battery drain.  I found people who used a lot of sunscreen, ate a lot of candy with Titanium dioxide, or wore sunglasses needed more frequent battery changes.

This was one of my early introductions to physics in neurosurgery.  I found that in PD patients with poor battery life often had co-morbid thyroid problems while being pale, and many were often on thyroid medication replacements.  It turns out low thyroid function also ruins battery function because it means melanin cannot do its job in the brain stem endogenously. These people also had perfuse sweating and heat intolerance  Why?

Melanin loses its electrical conductivity as heat is lost. This happens when melanin is not hydrated by CCO water production. ### Tweet 4

Picard mentions GDF -15 a lot in the pod but never links it to heme protein damage or evolution. I do. His eR principle directly scales to EPO biology. EPO infusions significantly increase GDF-15 levels, showing a strong positive correlation, as GDF-15 acts as an erythroid regulator, increasing with EPO to potentially dampen excessive red blood cell production (erythropoiesis) and influencing iron metabolism, making it a key marker for monitoring the effectiveness and impact of EPO therapy, especially in conditions like thalassemia.

GDF-15 is an autocrine/paracrine feedback signal, helping to control the overproduction of red blood cells stimulated by EPO.

GDF-15 levels strongly correlate with iron markers (like transferrin saturation, serum iron) and transfusion dependency, rising with more frequent transfusions.

While GDF-15 rises with EPO, it often shows an inverse relationship with hemoglobin levels, meaning it's higher when hemoglobin is lower, reflecting the body's effort to stimulate red blood cell production.

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1997296777112821964>

I blame Bernays for many of Woodrow Wilson's biggest errors in WW1 and setting the Stage for WW2 in Germany.

I believe Woodrow Wilson made several significant mistakes during his presidency, particularly regarding his handling of World War I and its aftermath, which had lasting consequences for both the United States and the world.

Key Mistakes Made by Woodrow Wilson

A. Failure to Prepare for War: Wilson underestimated the need for military preparedness as World War I escalated. He ignored calls from military leaders and politicians, like Theodore Roosevelt, to strengthen the U.S. military, believing that the U.S. could remain neutral and act as a mediator.

B. Illusions of Peace: Wilson clung to the belief that he could mediate peace between warring nations. His early attempts to broker peace were noble but ultimately unrealistic, as the war's brutality and the entrenched positions of the belligerents made meaningful negotiations impossible.

C. Vague 14 Points: His 14 Points, created with Bernays, outlined his vision for post-war peace, were criticized for being vague and unrealistic. This lack of clarity undermined their effectiveness and contributed to dissatisfaction among both allies and adversaries.

D. League of Nations: This horrible Idea became the UN after WW2. Wilson's push for the League of Nations, had good intentions, was poorly executed. He insisted on including the League's Covenant in the Treaty of Versailles, which ultimately led to its rejection by the U.S. Senate. He allowed two American Fabians loyal to the Crown argue on his behalf. The Dulles Brothers. This was a catastrophic error. Other critics agree with me as well that a more flexible approach in Germany could have led to a more favorable outcome for the League's acceptance. But the Crown wanted to punish Germany for WW1 and its brutality.

E. Ignoring Domestic Sentiment: Wilson failed to adequately educate the American public about the responsibilities and implications of entering the war. His lack of engagement with Congress and the public led to a disconnect that hampered support for his policies. A reliance on Bernays work was the main reason why.

F. Post-War Policies: After the war, Wilson's insistence on imposing a republican government on Germany and his handling of the peace negotiations alienated many former allies and contributed to instability in Europe, setting the stage for future conflicts.

G. Racial Policies: Domestically, Wilson's administration was marked by regressive racial policies, including the re-segregation of federal offices, which contradicted the progressive ideals he espoused. In other words, he did things that did not back up his words = BERNAYS 101 and it lead to the pictures below in the world.

The key is not waiting for the right time to learn the truth.  The key is ACTION now.  Just making a tiny change — be it good or bad — can guide our life to a very different destination.  This is what all of biology is based on too as the blog explains.  A small stimulus in a cell leads to massive amplification in tissues and systems in our bodies.  We just never get how non-linear stimuli work in nature because of our educational and societal myopia.

Consider the following example: If a pilot leaving from LAX adjusts the heading just 3.5 degrees south, you will land in Washington, D.C., instead of New York. Such a small change is barely noticeable at takeoff — the nose of the airplane moves just a few feet — but when magnified across the entire United States, you end up hundreds of miles apart…......and in the wrong city.

This is what Parity Violation did to ferredoxin and then ferredoxin lead to your evolution.  How is that for a tiny change leading to a massive change in life?

What is the problem with a world based on massive amounts of data?  You have to have the ability to critically think rapidly and decide. You can no longer need an infinite stretch of time ahead of you to start to think, infinite energy to make the smallest decision. The world is getting more dense because of data. Without the ability to think data become useless. The immense number of useless projects is bewildering. Data is now created now created for algorithms to use not our brain. Too many things are allowed to be put in to balance up an uncertain scale via a computer. You can't disappear anymore. You die in a state of total indecision, unless you learn to think critically.  This blog contains Genesis first data on how to decentrlaized your health.  How much do your experts know about Parity Violation and the polarization of light?

This idea argues for the power of trying to be just 1% better every day.  When will you realize the smallest changes in your life can change your life.  Go see the sunrise to get your 1% of UNPOLARIZED LIGHT today.

Greatness defined by action.

The leptin-melanocortin axis, a cornerstone of mammalian energy balance, traditionally viewed through a biochemical lens as a linear cascade of hormone-receptor interactions, harbors profound quantum photochemistry at its core; one that routinely violates kasha's rule. You should know that the eye is proximal to this pathways embedded in the central retinal pathways. This tells us evolution did something rather unusual to it for some environmental reason.

That reason occured 66 milions yrs ago when an asteroid blocked the sun with particulate matter. It changed the spectrum below on Earth. Life had to react to it. As a result neuropsin was innovated at this time.

Kasha's rule posits that photochemical reactions occur exclusively from the lowest excited singlet state (s₁) after rapid internal conversion from higher states (s₂, s₃), minimizing energy waste. yet, in this axis, anti-kasha effects dominate, where reactions proceed directly from higher excited states (s₂ or s₃), enabling ultrafast (femtosecond-scale) transformations that outpace vibrational relaxation and non-radiative decay.

This violation, far from an anomaly in modern mammals, is was a kinetic triumph, allowing selective control of signaling in the hypothalamus, where leptin binds LepRb receptors to activate pro-opiomelanocortin (pomc) neurons, yielding α-melanocyte-stimulating hormone (α-msh) for melanocortin-4 receptor (mc4r) activation, suppressing appetite while also boosting metabolism. This was needed when the sun spectrum was turned off. If you listened to the recent Huberman/Foisbury pod you'd see these two have zero physics backround to even posit this question, yet we know neuropsin sits in front of the entire leptin melanocortin pathway of ALL MAMMALS.

This made the recovery from the last extinction event speed up rapidly. How would this have helped the thermodynamic arrow of time post KT event? Do not ask Huberman or Fosbury. They remain deeply in the DARK because they still think life is about wavelengths exclusively when it is not. Polarization of light is way more important than wavelngth. These two just have piss running down their leg when you mention it to them. Hence why they are afraid to talk about real quantum biology. Physics > biology as a fundamental science.

Not only are they are actually expressed in the exact right place OPN3 (encephalopsin) is highly expressed in the arcuate nucleus, PVN, and other hypothalamic feeding centers (Blackshaw & Snyder, 1999; Halford et al., 2009; Buhr et al., 2015).

OPN5 (neuropsin) is also present in the hypothalamus and has been directly linked to seasonal and acute regulation of energy balance in mammals (including leptin sensitivity modulation).

They are genuine UV/blue-sensitive bistable photopigments in the dark brain Both OPN3 and OPN5 absorb maximally around 380–480 nm (well within the range of brain-generated chemiluminescence and scattered light in the median eminence).

They undergo real 11/13-cis all-trans retinal isomerization cycles in complete darkness, driven by ultra-weak biophoton fluxes or thermal isomerization (Tochitsky et al., 2017; Koyanagi et al., 2013).

Crucially, their photoisomerization quantum yield is extremely high (~0.3–0.65), and the primary event (isomerization + protein conformational change) occurs in <500 fs.

Documented physical and functional interaction with leptin signaling OPN3 knockout mice become obese and leptin-resistant on high-fat diet (Busnelli et al., 2018). Blue-light activation of hypothalamic OPN3 acutely suppresses feeding in mice within minutes — an effect that is completely absent in MC4R knockouts, proving it acts through the melanocortin axis (Baik et al., Nature 2020).

Leptin receptor long form (LepRb) and OPN3 co-immunoprecipitate in hypothalamic lysates (unpublished preprints 2022–2024 and private communications from multiple labs).

Anti-Kasha behavior is built-in Retinal itself is one of the textbook molecules that routinely violates Kasha’s rule: the reactive state for isomerization is the strongly allowed S₂ (¹Bᵤ) state, reached directly by blue-light absorption, and torsion occurs before significant vibrational relaxation to S₁. The effective reaction time from photon absorption to bond rotation is ~200–500 fs, with almost no heat dissipation.

My idea is the most plausible concrete mechanism (2025 state-of-the-art speculation) LepRb and OPN3 (or OPN5) form a stable heterodimeric complex on the intracellular face of the plasma membrane in POMC (and possibly AgRP) neurons.

Resting state (low leptin, famine mode): retinal in OPN3 is in the thermally stable 11-cis (or equivalent dark state) conformation → OPN3 cytoplasmic tail clamps STAT3 onto LepRb pTyr¹¹⁴² via an electrostatic latch.

Leptin binds → JAK2 autophosphorylation → creates a local electrostatic perturbation that slightly raises the energy of the OPN3 dark state. A single 380–450 nm photon (from catecholamine oxidation chemiluminescence or scattered light) hits the retinal → ultrafast S₂ → torsion → OPN3 undergoes a large-amplitude conformational change in <1 ps → the STAT3 latch is physically ripped open → STAT3 dissociates in picoseconds → immediate activation of PI3K and FoxO1 pathways → POMC “on”, AgRP “off” within seconds using pre-made neuropeptide pools.

No new genetic transcription is required for the acute flip — exactly as life would have needed for a femtosecond-effective switch.

My Verdict on Bob and Andy is beware of who packs your parachute.

My encephalopsin/neuropsin/melanopsin co-chromophore hypothesis is not fringe anymore in 2025 — it is the leading candidate mechanism for how non-image-forming light information modulates leptin sensitivity and feeding on minute-to-hour timescales. Fast-forward 65 million years to the dawn of mammals: evolution rigged a brilliant optical hack for survival. This duo, NO as the stem cell whisperer made by UV light and POMC as the melanin maestro, kept our hormone factories humming, ensuring all our sex steroid hormones flowed like a river for muscle, mood, and mating.

NO, born from heme cytochromes in mitochondria, acted as a decentralized messenger, diffusing through your mitochondrial network to depot stem cells and prime the POMC pump. Pregnenolone branches into testosterone for the lads (and estrogen/progesterone for the ladies), or cortisol for stress kicks. This cholesterol transport inside mitochondria? Mitochondria, those ancient ferredoxin descendants, became the gatekeepers: iron-sulfur clusters shuffling electrons, EMFs aligning cristae for optimal flow, and UPEs flashing the "all clear" for hormone assembly.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1994410666569138434>

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1994413845150892492>

In 1927, a quiet physics professor at the University of Queensland set up an experiment so strange, so slow, and so patient that it has outlived him, his students, and almost everyone who was alive when it began. And in nearly a century, not a single person has ever seen its defining event actually happen.

Thomas Parnell wanted to challenge his students’ assumptions about the physical world. One day, he held up a glossy black lump of pitch—solid as stone, brittle enough to shatter with a hammer. It looked completely immovable.

“This,” he told them, “is a liquid.”

When they laughed, he decided to show them.

Parnell heated the pitch until it softened like thick tar and poured it into a glass funnel. Then he waited three years for it to settle. In 1930, he cut the sealed stem and stepped back. Gravity would do the rest.

Eight years passed before the first drop finally fell in 1938. By then, his students were long gone, living lives far from the physics lab. The second drop fell in 1947—after a world war. The third in 1954. The fourth in 1962. Each drop descended only once every 8–9 years, moving at a rate 100 billion times slower than water.

But the strangest truth of all?
No one ever witnessed a drop fall. Not once. Not ever.

Not Thomas Parnell, who died in 1948 having never seen his experiment complete even a single cycle.

Not his successor, Professor John Mainstone, who took over in 1961. Mainstone watched the experiment for 52 years—half a century of checking, observing, hoping—yet every time a drop fell, he was away from the room by minutes. He missed one drop because he went to get coffee.

By the 2000s, technology was on their side. A camera was installed. Finally—finally—humanity would catch a drop in real time.

Except the camera angle was blocked.

The next drop? The footage corrupted.

It was as if the universe was playing a cosmic prank.

Today, the Pitch Drop Experiment is still running inside a glass dome at the University of Queensland—recognized as the longest continuously running lab experiment on Earth. A 24/7 livestream watches over it. Thousands of people keep their tabs open, waiting for a moment that might not happen for decades.

The tenth drop is forming now. Slowly. Patiently. Inevitably.

Here’s what Professor Parnell understood nearly a century ago:
Just because you can’t see progress doesn’t mean it isn’t happening.

Pitch flows. Mountains move. Continents drift millimeters a year. Coral reefs grow slowly, rings forming unseen. Blue light makes you gay and infertile. The most powerful forces in nature are almost invisible in real time. But even some wise Bitcoiners do not know their own Dunning Kruger moments. Be aware of this.

How do decentralized doctors visits begin? They start on Saturday and going on into Sunday's before sunset. Here is how they look.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1992648493190594790>

Why do we begin this way?

How does the sun link to endocrinology and how does the photo endocrine programming link to the nuclear genome to alter methylation of the DNA via the epigenomic software created by mitochondria in cells?

Vitamin D and Parathyroid Hormone are how this process operates in humans. Vitamin D, synthesized in the skin, and parathyroid hormone (PTH), secreted by the parathyroid glands, increase serum Ca2+ concentrations via actions of cells from the changes in mitochondria on the gut, kidney, and bone. These two calcemic hormones regulate cytochrome p450 27B1 (CYP27B1) gene expression directly. These are heme proteins and this is how the sun alters and affects the epigenetic system of mammals.

PTH activates CYP27B1 transcription through stimulation of protein kinases A and C (PKA and PKC), whereas vitamin D, through binding to its nuclear receptor, VDR, represses CYP27B1 (heme protein) transcription. The VDR receptor slows ECT speeds while red light in the sun continues to keep the ATPase spinning to move protons EVEN though the flow of electrons from food or the beta-oxidation of our fat stores slows.

This photonic protein wiring diagram reflects the vitamin D-mediated repression of the CYP27B1 gene, which results in rapid methylation of CpG sites on DNA and his is induced by vitamin D in this gene promoter. This methylation step requires DNMT1 and DNMT3B to be operational.

Conversely, treatment with PTH causes active CpG demethylation of the CYP27B1 promoter. Thus, these results obtained with the CYP27B1 promoter further suggest that DNA methylation/demethylation plays an important role in hormonal regulation of transcription. These are two of the many control arms of the epigenome in humans operational in sunlight. The environment is the key to epigenomic programming. Environmental factors induce numerous changes in DNA methylation, including imprinted genes involved in development. Regarding smoking, SP1 transcription factor activation by the nicotinic acetylcholine receptors and the increase of S-adenosylmethionine (the cell’s methyl donor) in response to hypoxia, as well as HIF-1α and methionine adenosyltransferase 2A, are involved.

It is important to note that the list of environmental factors capable of inducing epigenetic errors seems to be greatly underestimated. The most responsive change agent appears to be non-ionizing electromagnetic radiation on proteins given the latest NTP study on RF radiation risks in kids and adults using technology. Epigenetics is defined as heritable changes in gene activity and expression that occur without alteration in DNA sequence.

Histone modifications have been shown to function as a code that coordinates the recruitment of effector proteins and regulates chromatin structure, gene expression, and genome stability. DNA methylation is a biochemical process that begins with the solar programming of methyl donor groups. B12 and folate are two such photoreceptor in humans that appears to be critical to epigenetic program changes in humans. Methylation epigenetics concerns itself where a methyl group is added to the cytosine DNA nucleotides. Maintenance of DNA methylation by DNA methyltransferases is essential for normal mammalian development and long-term transcriptional silencing.

The conversion of 5-methylcytosine to 5-hydroxymetyhylcytosine by ten–eleven translocation enzymes (TETs) is the initial step of active DNA demethylation. Many diseases have been associated with differentially methylated regions, and demethylating agents or methyl donors are being evaluated as epigenetic therapies. Sunlight is likely the cheapest and most effective ones.

Vitamin D₃ → VDR-RXR heterodimer → genomic repression of CYP27B1 (the 1α-hydroxylase that makes active 1,25-D).

→ This is a classic light-driven negative/positive feedback loop that keeps serum Ca²⁺ and phosphate in the exact range mitochondria need for OxPhos, cristae geometry, and DDW production.

Red/NIR light keeps ATP synthase spinning and DDW flowing even when electron flow slows (exactly as you said) → maintains the methyl-donor pool (SAM) and the TET-enzyme oxidants (α-KG, O₂).

Blue light / nnEMF → CCO block → NO → electron backup → ROS → lipid peroxidation → ferroptosis → loss of matrix DDW → collapse of methyl-donor gradients → epigenetic chaos = all chronic diseases.

Hi Jack,

Wondering if you'd be able to break down what happened after I took Venlafaxine (Effexor) a few years ago and if there's any specific things needed to be done to hopefully repair any damage.

After what I believe was a spontaneous CSF leak that happened one night and started all my chronic issues I was diagnosed with vestibular migraine around 3 months later.

Venlafaxine 75mg was indicated for this so I was on it for around 12 months (give or take the 3 months wean I did).

Within 3 weeks my emotions went numb, I lost my libido, erections and ability to pretty much feel love or any strong emotion. There was a very definite and obvious "change" but I believe I still had the ability to focus and enjoy hobbies etc.

After deciding to taper off, I would get brain zaps and all the other jazz that comes with it. After fully tapering, I was then introduced to anhedonia. Unable to feel or enjoy anything, unable to focus on anything for too long without getting the irritable and feeling like I need to do something else etc. Essentially feel there's been a permanent change since starting this.

This has improved slightly but not even close to where I'd like to be. Add all the daily brain fog, memory lapses etc and my brain is running on empty.

Does this need anything specific additional to the core light, water & magnetism?

Listening to this months webinar you mentioned accutane extending recovery from 6 months up to 5 years. This prompted me to ask this question and about my accutane use in the future as this is a huge jump in recovery time and shows how powerful these drugs are.

Thanks,
Ja$%^

My thesis posits that life's core "code" resides in conserved correlations of phase, spin, and topology, geometric invariants that maintain coherence beyond mere voltage gradients. Man-made electromagnetic (EM) pollution disrupts this coherence, leading to health risks like aging, cancer, and failed regeneration. Voltage is merely a "courier," while true memory and form emerge from photonic organization of spin and topological symmetry. Venlafaxine, as a synthetic molecule introduced into this already polluted EM environment, would act to exacerbate disruptions by interfering with biological EM phenomena. Below, I list hypothesized reasons grounded in its structure, spectra, pharmacology, and potential EM interactions. These are imaginative extensions based on my published framework, drawing from known drug effects and bio-EM principles (e.g., biophotons, ion flows, and field coherence), while acknowledging that direct studies on venlafaxine-EM-biology intersections are limited because BigHarma isn't going to harpon its own product with truthful decentralized science.

What Likely Happened in Your case: A Breakdown of Events and Mechanisms

Your timeline starts with a spontaneous cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) leak, which can cause symptoms like headaches, dizziness, and neurological issues due to pressure changes in the brain/spine. This was diagnosed as vestibular migraine (common overlap, as both involve inner ear/brainstem dysregulation). Venlafaxine 75mg was prescribed, an SNRI that boosts serotonin and norepinephrine to stabilize mood and reduce migraine frequency.

Initial Effects (Within 3 Weeks: Emotional Numbness, Loss of Libido/Erections, Diminished Strong Emotions): Pharmacological Angle: Venlafaxine inhibits reuptake of serotonin (strongly) and norepinephrine (moderately), flooding synapses with these neurotransmitters. This can desensitize receptors over time, leading to emotional blunting (reduced dopamine signaling in reward pathways) and sexual dysfunction (via serotonin dampening arousal centers in the brain/spinal cord). It's a known side effect in 20-60% of users, often hitting early. Your "definite change" aligns with this, libido/erection issues stem from altered autonomic nervous system balance, while emotional numbness reflects hypofrontality (reduced prefrontal cortex activity).

During/After Tapering (Brain Zaps, Then Anhedonia, Irritability, Inability to Focus, Brain Fog, Memory Lapses):Pharmacological Angle: Tapering over 3 months is better than abrupt stop, but Venlafaxine's short half-life (~5 hours) often causes "discontinuation syndrome" in 50-80% of users: brain zaps (electrical-like sensations from rapid serotonin fluctuations), irritability, and rebound anxiety/depression. Post-taper, protracted withdrawal can emerge, lasting months/years in 10-20% of cases, manifesting as anhedonia (inability to feel pleasure), cognitive fog, and emotional flatness.

This is sometimes called Post-Antidepressant Syndrome or specifically Post-SSRI/SNRI Sexual Dysfunction (PSSD) for the persistent sexual/emotional components. Your CSF leak might have predisposed you to this outcome, as it could have already altered intracranial pressure and EM homeostasis, amplifying drug effects.

Tie to Thesis: Withdrawal "zaps" hint at disrupted bioelectric patterns, voltage instability detuning deeper coherence. Long-term, the drug's molecular interference (e.g., chiral enantiomers messing with spin-aligned processes) erodes topological invariants, leading to "permanent change" feel. In an EM-polluted world (as per Becker's warnings), this compounds, turning transient disruption into chronic detuning.

One thing that could help people with this problem is my breakdown of PDE4 inhibitors' core mechanisms, elevating cAMP to activate PKA/CREB and drive BDNF production for enhanced neuroplasticity, reduced neuroinflammation (e.g., via lower TNF-α and IL-6), and stress resilience.

It aligns well with established pathways. Animal models consistently show these effects translating to antidepressant outcomes, improved memory, and neuroprotection, often paralleling exercise-induced myokine benefits like BDNF, IGF-1, and VEGF surges. For symptoms like anhedonia/emotional numbness, this BDNF upregulation can restore reward circuitry disrupted by SNRIs like Venlafaxine, potentially aiding "field access" in my thesis framework.

Brain fog and memory lapses benefit from mitigated ER stress and oxidative damage, refining synaptic topology. Libido/erections tie into anti-inflammatory vascular support, with possible PSSD relief via neural symmetry, though direct human data is emerging. In EM-polluted environments,

PDE4 inhibition may counter nnEMF's potential BDNF suppression through quinolinic acid pathways (a kynurenine metabolite that exacerbates neurotoxicity via NMDA agonism and melanin disruption), while boosting AMPK/Sirt1 for mitochondrial biogenesis, mirroring BHB's HDAC inhibition to upregulate BDNF and plasticity. While nnEMF-BDNF links are more associative than causal in current literature, quinolinic acid's role in inflammation and BBB degradation supports my point on environmental stressors amplifying centralized disruptions.

### Tweet 21
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1991866160157966515>

The follow up: PDE4 inhibitors enhance endogenous signals like cAMP-mediated coherence, aligning with my emphasis on natural EM restoration over synthetic overrides. They bolster "grammar" for phase/spin/topology invariants by stabilizing network oscillations and synaptic efficacy (e.g., in CA3-CA1 circuits), potentially retuning detuned fields from drugs or EM pollution. The hippocampal subfields are four subfields CA1, CA2, CA3, and CA4 that make up the structure of the hippocampus.

PDE4 Inhibitors in Post-SSRI Injury CasesConsideration is reasonable for cognitive/mood symptoms in post-SSRI syndromes (e.g., PSSD, emotional blunting, anhedonia), given PDE4's antidepressant and plasticity-enhancing effects, but evidence for sexual dysfunction specifically is absent; no direct studies link them to PSSD treatment as of 2025.

PSSD involves persistent sexual (e.g., genital numbness, ED), cognitive, and emotional deficits post-SSRI/SNRI (e.g., Venlafaxine), potentially from serotonin/norepinephrine imbalances, epigenetic changes, or neuroinflammation.

Current treatments (e.g., bupropion, pramipexole, amphetamines) target dopamine, but outcomes vary; no PDE4 mentions in PSSD literature. PDE4 inhibitors (e.g., rolipram) show antidepressant activity in models by boosting BDNF/CREB, countering anhedonia and brain fog, potentially addressing "field access" disruptions from SSRIs. For libido/erections, anti-inflammatory vascular effects (via PDE5 parallels) and AMPK/Sirt1 activation might help, but this is speculative without trials.

Roflumilast/apremilast improve mood/cognition in other contexts (e.g., psoriasis, schizophrenia), suggesting off-label utility as a bridge in your decentralized protocol, synergizing with exercise/ketones for BDNF. Risks include GI upset; start low under MD supervision, especially post-Venlafaxine.

More research needed for PSSD-specific efficacy.

# Thread: I just gave a talk to a group of medical students..........Here was my only slide.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1991212270202802525.html>
**Published:** 2025-11-21T13:34:34.000Z
**Tweets:** 9
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1991212270202802525>

I just gave a talk to a group of medical students..........Here was my only slide.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1991212838753341835>

Here was the substance of the talk.

The talk was a sobering discussion for me, yet it empowered my reflection on navigating a world where societal dogma often clashes with truth.

I explored how civilization rewards comfortable lies while penalizing painful truths.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1991213025521520890>

Now we’ll focus on the hope for humanity amidst this tension, the challenges of earning a living, and our personal choices in deciding where we fit into this complex landscape.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1991213386210898245>

The Hope of Decentralized Scientific Revolution

Let’s start with a glimmer of hope: when society’s dogma strays too far from reason, history shows that a scientific revolution often follows. Think of Copernicus, who upended the geocentric model of the universe, or Darwin, who challenged religious dogma with evolution. These revolutions realign our collective understanding with nature, bringing us closer to the truth. But the process is long and painful. Revolutionaries are often tormented—or worse. Copernicus published his heliocentric theory on his deathbed to avoid persecution. Galileo was placed under house arrest.

More recently, during the COVID-19 era, dissenting scientists/clinicians who questioned official narratives, like the origins of the virus or vaccine mandates, faced censorship, career ruin, and public vilification, with no accountability for those who enforced the dogma. Currently, no arrests have been made for what some see as overreaches or “crimes against nature” during that time.

The old guard rarely admits it was wrong. Institutions, whether governments, scientific bodies, or industries, double down, protecting their power and investments. This resistance delays progress, leaving truth-seekers to bear the burden of pushing humanity forward. It’s a brutal cycle, but it’s also a testament to the resilience of those who prioritize reality over dogma.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1991213826918830406>

Earning a Living Amidst the Chaos: A Way to Force Compliance.

In the meantime, we all face a practical reality: to earn a living. This is where the tension between truth and dogma hits home. As discussed earlier, a career often means aligning your efforts with society’s demands, not truth. If society rewards lies, like promoting profitable but harmful products, you might thrive by going along. But if you challenge the system, like Andrew Marino did with EMFs in Going Somewhere, you risk everything. The world penalizes truth-tellers because they threaten the stability of the dogmatic structures that civilization relies on.

This creates a dilemma. We’re all trying to survive while hoping that civilization hasn’t strayed so far from nature that it triggers extinction. Climate change, biodiversity loss, and the health impacts of non-native EMFs or industrial toxins are real concerns, some argue we’re already on the brink. I don't. As I've noted, the stakes are high in some areas, and the gap between dogma and reality is widening because of surveillance technocracy. People are losing faith in institutions, but many lack the tools to think independently, making recovery uncertain.

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1991214521919447129>

Doing the Math: Where Do You Fit In?

So, how do you navigate this? It’s about “doing the math” and plugging in the variables of where you want to fit into this messy reality. This isn’t a literal equation—it’s a metaphor for reasoning through your choices with clarity and purpose. You have a spectrum of options, and where you land depends on your values, skills, and tolerance for risk.

On one end, you could be the next Aristotle or Copernicus, a revolutionary who challenges dogma head-on, pushing humanity toward truth. This path is noble but grueling. You’ll face resistance, possibly torment, as history shows. You might innovate in medicine, exposing flaws in current practices, or biophysics, revealing the actual impacts of EMFs. But the cost could be your career, social standing, or safety.

Alternatively, you might lie low in a remote cave, metaphorically or literally. Plato wrote the book on that. This means opting out of the system, minimizing exposure to its dogmas, and focusing on personal survival or small-scale impact. You might live off the grid, grow your food, and avoid the noise of conventional wisdom. It’s safer, but it limits your ability to influence broader change.

Most of us will fall somewhere in between, balancing the need to earn a living with the desire to align with truth. Maybe you work within the system but carve out space for independent thought, practicing first-principles thinking to filter dogma while still paying the bills.

Or perhaps you find a niche where you can quietly innovate, embrace decentralized money, like developing sustainable technologies that don’t threaten the old guard too directly. In private, you'll see to replace the old paradigm.

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1991215287002161314>

Thinking as a SAVAGE for Your Sovereignty

This is where the idea of thinking as a “SAVAGE” for your sovereignty comes in. “SAVAGE” here likely stands for a mindset, someone who is Self-Aware, Vigilant, Adaptable, Grounded, and Empowered. It’s about reclaiming your mental sovereignty by thinking independently, grounding yourself in nature, and filtering out societal noise. This aligns with the epistemological framework I’ve discussed: using cognitive filters to evaluate ideas, ensuring internal consistency and external alignment with reality.

When you think this way, you don’t need a premed counselor or counselor to tell you what to do. You become capable of reasoning through your choices, whether becoming a revolutionary, retreating to a cave, or finding a middle path. You’ll see through the dogmas that reward lies, penalize truths, and make decisions that protect your integrity and well-being.

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1991215585917575632>

Why This Matters Now

This perspective isn’t something you’ll hear from a typical college advisor. They’ll likely steer you toward a career that fits society’s mold, get a degree, join the system, and don’t rock the boat. But as civilization’s dogma drifts further from reality, that path becomes riskier.

As I've mentioned, societal norms' “security blanket” is unraveling. The cracks are showing, whether it’s the fallout from COVID policies, environmental crises, or technological overreach.

Thinking as a SAVAGE gives you the tools to navigate this uncertainty, to earn a living without losing your connection to truth, and to contribute to humanity’s recovery, if recovery is still possible.

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1991216067725640054>

Final Thoughts

The hope for humanity lies in scientific revolutions that realign us with reason. Still, these come at a steep cost to the revolutionaries, as we’ve seen from Copernicus to COVID dissenters. In the meantime, we must earn a living while hoping civilization hasn’t committed too many crimes against nature to recover.

This means “doing the math”, reasoning through where you fit, whether as a revolutionary, a hermit, or something in between.

Thinking as a SAVAGE for your sovereignty empowers you to make those choices independently, grounding yourself in nature rather than dogma. It’s a path to mental freedom in a world that often punishes truth, and it’s more critical now than ever as society teeters on the edge.

Think carefully about joining me today in decentralizing medicine; let’s keep reasoning, adapting, and seeking truth, wherever our paths may lead!

What don't sunglass and eye glass manufacturer's know? Polarized light and lenses are used as a tool to detect and measure the tiny effects of Parity Violation in atoms and molecules, where the weak force causes a slight "handedness" in physical systems that are otherwise mirror-symmetric.

The core link is that the weak force's violation of parity symmetry leads to a mixing of atomic or molecular states that should, under normal circumstances (governed by the parity-conserving electromagnetic and strong forces), have a definite, opposite parity. This mixing is extremely small but results in two primary observable effects involving polarized light. It affects water networks and all proteins that interact with the polarized light.

Note: all artificial sources of light are POLARIZED.

When linearly polarized light passes through an atomic vapor or a chiral medium like the human eye, its plane of polarization rotates slightly. The amount of rotation is related to the degree of parity violation in the atoms distal to it.

Materials exposed to the weak interaction can show a minuscule difference in how they absorb left-handed versus right-handed circularly polarized light. Why is this a big deal in my human patients?

Stellar masses are designed to use you as their lens. This is why DNA codes only for semiconductive proteins that all have absorption and emission spectra.

Parity vioation in your tissues explain life's universal preference for L-amino acids and D-sugars, a puzzle since Pasteur's 1848 discovery of molecular chirality. when you wear lenses in front of your eyes or bath in light man makes you just broke a fundamental law of Nature and it always leads to DISEASE your doctors are impotent to repair.

### Tweet 4

Light itself can be polarized, meaning its electric field oscillates in a specific orientation (linear, circular, etc.). Circularly polarized light has a specific "handedness" (left or right).

By using highly precise measurements of how a material interacts differently with left- vs. right-circularly polarized light (circular dichroism) or the rotation of linearly polarized light (optical rotation), scientists can measure this minute, weak-force-induced asymmetry.
